title: Can we enjoy Rebuild Live Act when we know the basic plot
display_title: Can we enjoy Rebuild Live Act when we know the basic plot
wikitext:
[]: Can we enjoy Rebuild/Live Act when we know the basic plot??             Quote Post by V » Fri May 11, 2007 7:14 am Can we enjoy Rebuild and the live action movies when we already know the basic plot? Again, I preface this as: I started as a Lord of the Rings fan. I read the books as a kid in the mid-90's, like 2-3 years before word of the movie adaptations came out. So I'm the last of the wave of fans who treated it as a given that "there's no way in hell you could adapt a book this complex into a movie" (this was back in the day when the concept of studios willing to film three, 3-hour movies, back to back in one consecutive shooting schedule, was unheard of) Now, I could appreciate LOTR as a movie...even though I knew the plot...because it had previously only existed as a book. And of course they had to change a lot (dialogue is not one-for-one corresponding with the book; they're condensations of dialogue. They do sprinkle in fun direct quotes from time to time, but it's still fresh; other adaptations have failed where they try to just have actors read off the page). However, anime is different from books; heck even manga are different from books: there is already VISUALLY a version of "Evangelion". Oh LOTR had concept art and stuff, a lot of it for decades, but never "this is what Eva-02 looks like when it jumps from battleship to battleship", or "this is what Asuka looks like when fighting Zeruel" of course, beyond this we've also got that we have actual, movie-scripted dialogue and voice actors in our minds; now the actual voice actors can be juggled around; that is, Rebuild could use some of the originals and the live action cast will just be "new" voice actors in a sense.... ...but what I'm saying is that we've already seen the story acted out, "live" in a sense, as a moving picture. ***Like I said I only really started watching Eva when it ran on Adult Swim but became instantly obsessed and I've been soaking up information ever since, but....can we get as hyped about "omg, Live Action Eva Trilogy" as for "live action LOTR"?? I mean some of you guys have been fans for what, 10 frakking years? (well not that many) I mean what gives Eva its staying power? It is a damn fine show and everything that followed it was just knockoffs, but still. I dunno; it's just weird in that "big movie adaptations with an entrenched fanbase" tend to be from books, not animation. But will we "fall in love all over again", as they say? That is I like "Aragorn in the book" and "Aragorn in the films" as two distinct characters; I like them both, and I think movie-Aragorn complements book-Aragorn really well, but I still appreciate movie-Aragorn on a level I did not anticipate. Hmmm....I guess it's how Harry Potter fans (well, the original ones) had "Hermione Granger" as an idea in their heads, before being told "she looks like Emma Watson" before the series even ended.

[]: Re: Can we enjoy Rebuild/Live Act when we know the basic plo             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri May 11, 2007 8:18 am V wrote: Now, I could appreciate LOTR as a movie...even though I knew the plot...because it had previously only existed as a book.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri May 11, 2007 6:39 pm Um.....the animated movies are generally considered one of the greatest tragedies of bad adaptation. I mean people point at those and go "this is an example of a bad adaptation". I do not want to start debating that though.

[]:             Quote Post by Executor » Fri May 11, 2007 6:43 pm It's a fucking reboot, can they really deviate from the original story so much? V: The Animated LOTR movies are pure fucking shit. Cant belive I wasted a fiver buying that crap.

[]: Re: Can we enjoy Rebuild/Live Act when we know the basic plo             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri May 11, 2007 8:18 pm V wrote: Um.....the animated movies are generally considered one of the greatest tragedies of bad adaptation.

[]:             Quote Post by Executor » Fri May 11, 2007 8:58 pm Yeah, it's not a good idea to deride it when it hasent even really gone into implement phase yet.

[]:             Quote Post by Knoxer » Fri May 11, 2007 9:20 pm Well look at it this way, Death note is a great anime... and also has a great live action movie as well. While some things are different it's good all the same. The Eva LA movie if they do it right, shouldn't be a total flop. Rather a great success.

[]:             Quote Post by Shwiggie » Fri May 11, 2007 11:32 pm Let's ask ourselves a couple simple questions. Did you enjoy Evangelion the first time you watched it? What about the second time? The third? Well, just how many times did you watch it, anyway?! Consider also the End of Evangelion film. You kinda/sorta knew what happened after episode 24 based on the oft-reviled 25-26. Did the movie help you appreciate aspects of the series more or less than before? And did your having watched the series cause you to appreciate EoE more or less? I can't wait for the new stuff to finally arrive. I'm betting that these projects will be more like EoE for us than another Death/Rebirth-style recap.

[]:             Quote Post by Opteron-O3 » Fri May 11, 2007 11:46 pm I'm still waiting for them to say something about Eva Live Action...

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat May 12, 2007 12:17 am Opteron-O3 wrote: I'm still waiting for them to say something about Eva Live Action...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat May 12, 2007 6:11 am Is this thread actually about Rebuild, at all?

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sat May 12, 2007 7:48 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Opteron-O3 wrote: I'm still waiting for them to say something about Eva Live Action...

[]:             Quote Post by Executor » Sat May 12, 2007 8:27 am Reichu wrote: Is this thread actually about Rebuild, at all?
